editorial_reasoning.dominant_topic: NFL Draft content leads, but NBA tanking has the funniest content

candidates_considered.hook: Projected #1 pick wants to stay home with family in Miami
NBA champion coach goes to college for $50M
Kings literally fouled Seth Curry on purpose while winning
Patriots coach caught with reporter, both married
Super Bowl winner adds another ring

candidates_considered.story: KD vs Dillon Brooks trash talk
candidates_considered.hook: KD dropped him after the comment

stories.headline: Fernando Mendoza Is Skipping the NFL Draft to Stay Home
stories.editorial_direction: This is actually a sweet story disguised as draft news. Lead with the uniqueness (projected #1 pick staying home), then get into the Miami connection (won title there, family there, mom has MS). The Raiders already learning his playbook angle is wild. Compare to other #1 picks who skipped.

tweets.text: Projected No. 1 overall pick Fernando Mendoza has informed the NFL that he is not planning to attend the NFL Draft in Pittsburgh this month, per sources. Mendoza wants to share the draft experience with his family in Miami.
The last time a No. 1 overall pick QB did not attend the NFL Draft was in 2021, when the Jaguars selected Trevor Lawrence.

More about Fernando Mendoza, the projected No. 1 pick in the NFL draft, informing the league that he is not planning to attend the draft:

https://www.espn.com/nfl/story/_/id/48421461/sources-fernando-mendoza-not-planning-attend-nfl-draft

key_stats: <cite index="1-14,8-4">Led Indiana to undefeated season and national title, beating Miami in Miami's home stadium</cite>
<cite index="5-11,5-12">3,535 passing yards, 41 TDs, 6 INTs, won Heisman Trophy in 2025</cite>
<cite index="3-15,3-16">Last #1 pick to skip draft was Travon Walker in 2022, stayed in Atlanta hotel</cite>
personal_angle: <cite index="8-4">Mendoza's mother Elsa has lived with multiple sclerosis for over 15 years and uses wheelchair</cite>
<cite index="1-12,1-13,1-14">Miami is where he grew up dreaming of being a pro QB, and where he won the national title against Miami</cite>
raiders_context: <cite index="4-5,4-7,4-8">Mendoza already learning Raiders offense with Brian Griese, installing playbook like Joe Burrow did</cite>
<cite index="4-11">Raiders signed Kirk Cousins for $20M guaranteed, may not start Mendoza right away</cite>
historical_context: <cite index="3-17,3-18">Last three #1 picks (Bryce Young, Caleb Williams, Cam Ward) all attended draft</cite>
<cite index="3-25,3-26">Every #1 pick in 2000s attended, this is more recent trend</cite>
draft_logistics: <cite index="2-4,2-9">Raiders hold #1 pick, draft April 23-25 in Pittsburgh</cite>
<cite index="7-10">NFL invitation restrictions make it hard to bring teammates to ceremony</cite>
scouting_report: <cite index="5-9,5-17">6-foot-5, 225 pounds, 79.2% adjusted completion percentage ranked 2nd nationally</cite>
<cite index="5-21,5-22">Played 97% of snaps in shotgun, needs work under center for NFL</cite>
where_it_goes: Draft in 15 days, Raiders visit Tuesday, will celebrate with family in Miami instead of Pittsburgh stage moment

stories.headline: Michael Malone Leaves NBA Championship for $50 Million College Experiment
stories.editorial_direction: This is UNC going full pro model after Belichick. Focus on the money ($8.3M/year), the NBA-to-college trend, and his daughter already being at UNC. The firing from Denver adds intrigue.

tweets.text: Michael Malone has officially signed a six-year contract worth more than $50 million dollars plus incentives to be the next head coach at North Carolina, per release.

"I have a chance to be a part of something special. ... This was the only college job I'd ever consider." 

Michael Malone on his decision to become the head coach of @UNC_Basketball.

money_details: <cite index="16-6,16-7,16-8">6-year, $50M contract averaging $8.33M/year, only Bill Self makes more among public school coaches</cite>
<cite index="16-18,16-19">UNC committed $6.75M annually in revenue sharing to men's basketball</cite>
nba_background: <cite index="17-17,18-15">Won 2023 NBA championship with Denver, franchise's all-time winningest coach</cite>
<cite index="19-1,19-9">Fired by Nuggets last spring with less than a week left in regular season</cite>
unc_connection: <cite index="12-6,15-5">His daughter Bridget is a sophomore volleyball player for the Tar Heels</cite>
<cite index="11-19,15-6">Observed UNC's first 5-6 practices last October, spoke to team after practice</cite>
pro_model_trend: <cite index="17-22,16-24">Joins Bill Belichick in UNC's pro coaching experiment, both from championship backgrounds</cite>
<cite index="19-5,19-15">UNC typically keeps job within 'Carolina Family', this is major departure from tradition</cite>
coaching_resume: <cite index="18-7,18-8">510 regular season wins, 44 postseason wins (20 more than any other Denver coach)</cite>
<cite index="15-12,15-13">College assistant experience but hasn't coached college since 2001</cite>

stories.headline: The NBA Tanking Epidemic Has Reached Peak Absurdity
stories.editorial_direction: Lead with the Kings fouling Seth Curry on purpose while WINNING. This isn't even trying to hide it anymore. Draymond calling it out adds legitimacy. Adam Silver's concerns about gambling implications.

tweets.text:
We have NEVER seen this level of tanking bruh 

Doug Christie literally instructed his players on the Kings TO FOUL SETH CURRY ON PURPOSE while up 101-100 in the 4th quarter 

INSANE 😭💀

Draymond Green about the tanking epidemic: 

"I saw a team tonight foul Seth Curry with three minutes to go in the game for no reason."

On what should be done about it: 

"Keep fining teams."

Every teams leading scorer tonight:

BKN — EJ Liddell
UTA — Kennedy Chandler
IND — Ethan Thompson
CHI — Rob Dillingham
MIN — Ayo Dosunmu 
LAL — Rui Hachimura
WAS — Bilal Coulibaly
TOR — Scottie Barnes 
MIA — Andrew Wiggins
MIL — AJ Green
NOP — Jeremiah Fears
DAL — Cooper Flagg
LAC — Kawhi Leonard
OKC — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander
PHX — Devin Booker
HOU — Kevin Durant

Final week of the regular season.

kings_example: <cite index="24-24,24-25,24-26,24-28">Alex Sarr fouled out in 22 minutes with 5 fouls in first 4 minutes of 3rd quarter, coach didn't pull him</cite>
<cite index="24-31,24-32">Wizards benched Coulibaly (19 min) and Carrington (26 min) entire 4th quarter while losing lead</cite>
draymond_reaction: Doug Christie fouled Seth Curry intentionally while leading 101-100 with 3 minutes left
Draymond called it unprecedented level of obvious tanking
silver_concerns: <cite index="24-17,24-18,24-19">Adam Silver says decision needs to be made at ownership level, has 'integrity implications' for gambling</cite>
<cite index="24-16">Third of fan bases actively rooting against their teams for two months</cite>
systemic_problem: <cite index="30-1,30-2">Volume of teams tanking unprecedented, full-blown race to bottom despite flattened odds</cite>
Tankathon odds show multiple teams competing for worst record simultaneously

stories.headline: Mike Vrabel and Married Reporter Caught in Arizona Hotel Scandal
stories.editorial_direction: Report the facts, no commentary. Both married to other people, caught at luxury hotel in Sedona. Vrabel married since 1999, Russini since 2020. Let the details speak for themselves.

tweets.text:
Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel and New York Times NFL reporter Dianna Russini were seen holding hands, hugging, and spending time together at a luxury hotel in Sedona, Arizona.

Both are married. TO OTHER PEOPLE 😳

Mike Vrabel has been married to Jen Vrabel since 1999.

Dianna Russini has been married to Kevin Goldschmidt, a Shake Shack executive, since 2020.

basic_facts: Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel and NY Times reporter Dianna Russini seen at luxury Sedona hotel
Both married to other people - Vrabel since 1999, Russini since 2020
Seen holding hands and hugging according to reports
professional_context: Vrabel is Patriots head coach in his first season
Russini covers NFL for New York Times
Potential conflict of interest between coach and reporter covering league

Hall of Famer Steve McMichael, a key member of the dominating defense that helped the 1985 Bears win the Super Bowl, has been diagnosed with chronic traumatic encephalopathy, the Concussion & CTE Foundation said.

Spurs star Victor Wembanyama is listed doubtful to play in Wednesday's game against Portland due to a rib contusion but there's confidence he will play at least one of the team's final three regular-season games.

Hitters with 20+ strikeouts and a batting average under .200 this season:

— Big Dumper

End of list.

JJ Redick said Deandre Ayton can't catch 😭💀

"He's had trouble catching the ball, so we've ran a bunch of plays for him, he's just had trouble catching the ball"

Bronny FC, our time is now. (Via @TickPick)
Jon Rahm skipping it off the water and in the hole on the 16th before the Masters will always be a must-watch 😳
